outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the social determinants of health, health inequalities and the role of nursing in public health
Critically evaluate evidence underpinning public health approaches and their application to nursing practice
Communicate effectively with peers in a collaborative group presentation setting
Demonstrate awareness of professional responsibilities, ethical considerations and societal influences in public health nursing
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of human anatomy, physiology and body systems relevant to nursing practice
Apply understanding of homeostasis, pathophysiology and biosciences to the assessment of nursing care needs
Use anatomical and physiological knowledge to underpin clinical decision-making
Demonstrate commitment to building and maintaining scientific knowledge as a foundation for evidence-based nursing
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of evidence-based practice and the research underpinning safe and effective nursing
Critically appraise research evidence and apply findings to inform nursing care and clinical decision-making
Apply evidence-based knowledge to the care of people with commonly encountered physical health conditions
Commit to lifelong learning and keeping knowledge and skills up to date through engagement with evidence

title: Collaborative Care Planning
note: Unit specification PDF was not accessible – outcomes and NMC mappings are indicative based on unit title and programme context

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge of collaborative care planning principles and person-centred approaches
Apply collaborative and interprofessional working principles to nursing care planning
Demonstrate person-centred communication and collaborative care planning skills
Work effectively with others and demonstrate professional accountability in the care planning process
Demonstrate foundational knowledge and understanding of communication and relationship management in nursing contexts
Apply clinical skills and nursing procedures in simulated practice settings
Demonstrate professional values and accountability appropriate to a student nurse on simulated placement
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the professional, ethical and values-based foundations of nursing practice, including The Code
Critically analyse the values, ethics and regulatory frameworks that underpin professional nursing identity
Demonstrate person-centred, values-based care in interactions with patients, families and colleagues
Demonstrate personal integrity, ethical conduct and cultural competence as an emerging nursing professional
Demonstrate understanding of the emerging role of the nurse and professional growth, including reflective practice
Demonstrate the ability to reflect analytically on personal development and produce a professional development plan
Demonstrate essential drug dosage calculation competence using the SafeMedicate platform
Demonstrate professional maturity, self-directed learning and effective communication
Demonstrate and apply knowledge of biosciences, pathophysiology and pharmacology to support clinical decision-making
Apply bioscientific knowledge to critically analyse nursing assessment and care planning
Respond proactively to signs of deterioration and apply bioscience knowledge to clinical nursing practice
Demonstrate commitment to applying scientific knowledge to safe, evidence-based nursing practice

title: Mental Health and Mental Ill Health in Children and Young People

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of mental health, mental ill health and the factors affecting the mental wellbeing of children and young people
Critically analyse evidence relating to mental health assessment, care planning and therapeutic approaches for CYP
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to support CYP with mental health challenges in practice
Demonstrate cultural competence, advocacy and collaborative working in the context of CYP mental health

title: Developing Knowledge and Skills for Nursing Practice (Semester 1)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate and apply knowledge of commonly encountered health conditions, medicines and treatments when undertaking nursing assessments and developing person-centred care plans
Critically analyse evidence to inform nursing interventions for people with complex care needs
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to provide evidence-based nursing care including nutrition, hydration, skin integrity, symptom management and discharge planning
Work effectively in teams, demonstrate professional values and contribute to the interdisciplinary care of people

title: Developing Knowledge and Skills for Nursing Practice (Semester 2)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate and apply knowledge of commonly encountered health conditions, medicines and treatments when undertaking nursing assessments and developing person-centred care plans
Critically analyse evidence to inform nursing interventions for people with complex care needs
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to provide evidence-based nursing care including nutrition, hydration, skin integrity, symptom management and discharge planning
Work effectively in teams, demonstrate professional values and contribute to the interdisciplinary care of people
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of dementia, its impact on individuals, families and carers, and the legal, ethical and governance frameworks underpinning dementia care
Critically evaluate creative and empowering approaches to dementia care within the evidence base
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to provide person-centred, creative and compassionate care for people living with dementia
Demonstrate empathy, cultural competence and advocacy in dementia care

title: Principles of Adult Critical and Intensive Care Nursing

outcomes.text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the principles, pathophysiology and clinical challenges encountered in critical and intensive care settings
Critically evaluate the evidence base underpinning nursing assessment and care in critical care environments
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills required for nursing care in a critical care context
Demonstrate professional accountability, working collaboratively in complex clinical situations and respond to emergencies

title: Nurse as a Facilitator of Learning

outcomes.text: Demonstrate a clear understanding of a range of learning styles and factors impacting on learning (motivation/environment). Have a critical appreciation of teaching and learning methods and select appropriate methods for learner, content and context. Demonstrate critical understanding of patient/client motivation for learning utilising relevant health psychology theories. Demonstrate critical knowledge of tools and resources that can enhance learning experience. Explore and understand issues that may enhance or create barriers to learning.

Demonstrate understanding of the roles and responsibilities of practice supervisors and practice assessors and their contribution to student assessment and progression. Provide clear instructions and explanations when supervising, teaching or appraising others. Give unambiguous, constructive feedback about strengths, weaknesses and potential for improvement. Encourage colleagues that helps them to reflect on their practice.

Develop a teaching session based on appropriate teaching and learning methods. Be able to reflect on a learning environment and what facilitates learning. Utilise research and evidence-based practice in the choice of learning opportunity and delivery of teaching to clients and families. Be able to reflect on effectiveness of own teaching style in relation to pedagogical theory. Identify the value of a range of tools to enhance learning using IT and innovative approaches.

Identify a learning opportunity and plan a teaching session for patient/client, carer, student, staff or relevant others. Deliver a teaching session to patients, clients or their families, carers, students, staff or relevant others. Demonstrate confidence in formal and informal styles of teaching.

Enhanced IT skills and use of other technologies. Selection of appropriate communication strategies to support client understanding. Skills in communication with other professional groups. Enhanced skills in reflection on own performance.

title: Nurse as Inquirer of Clinical Evidence (NICE)

outcomes.text: Demonstrate a broad understanding of health and social care philosophy and policy, and its translation into ethical and evidence-based nursing practice. Demonstrate the ability to effectively retrieve and appraise a range of data/information that underpins and informs the evidence base for nursing practice with specific reference to their own field of practice. Demonstrate a coherent understanding of the key aspects of nursing practice through critical analysis and synthesis of information/data from a wide range of sources, at least some of which is at the forefront of the discipline.

Critically apply the knowledge and understanding to the management and delivery of competent nursing care in a variety of clinical practice settings and across the patient/client dependency continuum. Critically appraise the evidence base for nursing interventions and consider strategies for further research and/or practice development with specific reference to their own field of practice.
Undertake procedures for planning, provision and management of person-centred nursing care using evidence-based best practice approaches.
Act with professional integrity, demonstrating fitness to practice, purpose and achievement of the NMC standards for pre-registration nursing.
Understand the principles of effective leadership, management, group and organisational dynamics and culture and apply these to team working and decision making.
Understand and apply the principles of human factors, environmental factors and strength-based approaches when working in teams.
Understand the principles and application of processes for performance management and how these apply to the nursing team.

Demonstrate an understanding of the roles, responsibilities and scope of practice of all members of the nursing and interdisciplinary team and how to make best use of the contributions of others involved in providing care.
Apply leadership principles in clinical practice and demonstrate effective clinical decision-making with appropriate escalation.
Deliver high-quality person-centred care and coordinate patient pathways across complex care settings.
Demonstrate professional responsibility and accountability in clinical practice and contribute to continuous improvement.
Understand the principles of effective leadership, management, group and organisational dynamics and culture and apply these to team working and decision making.
Understand and apply the principles of human factors, environmental factors and strength-based approaches when working in teams.
Lead and manage nursing care with increasing autonomy and demonstrate advanced clinical decision-making.
Provide and evaluate high-quality person-centred care with confidence and manage complex patient needs.
Demonstrate leadership qualities and collaborative working to support quality improvement and safe practice.
Understand the principles of effective leadership, management, group and organisational dynamics and culture and apply these to team working and decision making.
Demonstrate autonomous leadership in clinical practice and advanced management of complex patient cohorts.
Lead safe, high-quality, person-centred nursing care and coordinate care across multiple settings.
Demonstrate fitness to practice as a registered professional nurse with accountability and integrity.

title: Advancing Care - The Social and Political Context of Health and Social Care

outcomes.text: Demonstrate a critical awareness of the social processes and socio-economic features of society, concepts of social exclusion, individual functioning and the differential demand/need for nursing and social care services. Demonstrate a critical understanding of the service delivery context in which nursing/health and social care operates.

Provide an analysis of the ethics underpinning nursing work and apply ethical principles and values to guide professional practice. Apply research evidence and show the development of research mindedness.
Conduct policy analysis and evaluate developments in health and social care systems. Use evidence to inform understanding of policy implementation and impact.
Reflect critically on the application of policy knowledge and evidence to nursing practice.
Apply policy knowledge and research evidence to inform person-centred care decisions. Communicate complex policy and contextual information to diverse audiences in written and digital formats.

Demonstrate verbal and written communication skills to explain policy implications to colleagues and service users. Demonstrate critical thinking in evaluating policy impact on nursing practice. Collaborate effectively across professional and service boundaries.

title: Nurse as a Leader, Manager and Coordinator of Care

outcomes.text: Understand the principles of effective leadership, management, group and organisational dynamics and culture and apply these to team working and decision making. Demonstrate effective person and team management by active listening and selecting appropriate strategies for positive outcomes of interaction.

Understand and apply the principles of human factors and strength-based approaches. Understand health economics and safe staffing principles. Understand the role of supervision in developing and maintaining professional competence. Understand person-centred approaches to care management.
Understand principles of ethical care coordination and evidence-based leadership practice.

Apply leadership and management principles to coordinate multidisciplinary team working. Manage team performance and reflect on effectiveness of care delivery. Evaluate quality improvement strategies and influence policy implementation through evidence.
Demonstrate reflective and critical thinking in leadership decision-making and team facilitation.

Manage digital systems effectively in coordinating care. Plan and manage safe discharge pathways. Coordinate and manage complex care transitions. Provide person-centred advocacy. Implement risk management and incident reflection processes.

Apply IT and communication skills effectively in leadership roles. Facilitate teamwork and collaborative care coordination. Demonstrate leadership qualities that foster professional development and safe culture.

Demonstrate critical appreciation of the current political and professional issues in contemporary nursing practice and education. Evidence the development of enhanced depth in understanding and application of the reflective practice theory. Demonstrate critical and comprehensive understanding of the role of the nurse and his or her own personal growth and development as a registered practitioner.
Demonstrate advanced drug calculation competence at injectable level.
Demonstrate critical reflection on professional development and leadership potential. Apply reflective theory to analyse professional role modelling and exemplary practice.
Demonstrate advanced drug calculation reasoning with justification.
Maintain autonomous portfolio development demonstrating professional growth. Demonstrate independent practice and professional maturity. Manage advanced medication measurement competences.
Demonstrate enhanced communication skills in professional contexts. Apply independent study strategies to continuous professional development. Demonstrate advanced reflection on role development.
